name: Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter
description: The Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter is a silver-impregnated ceramic tabletop filter.

taxonomy.sub_category: Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter Updated on December 7, 2023 · Created on September 27, 2018 Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter The Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter is a silver-impregnated ceramic tabletop filter. Developed By Envirogard Products Ltd Tested By Canadian Standards Association (CSA) University of Arizona Content Partners Centre for Affordable Water and Sanitation Technologies (CAWST) Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Rainfresh MP4U Gravity Filter is a tabletop filter produced by the Canadian company Envirogard Products Ltd . and distributed in disaster response scenarios by Global Medic . The filter setup consists of four silver-impregnated ceramic cartridges, two buckets, a spigot for dispensing water, a filter gauge, cleaning mesh, and the necessary hardware for installation. The user fills the top bucket with dirty water and lets the clean water pass through the filters and into the bottom bucket at a max flow rate of 3 liters per hour. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy Market Suggested Retail Price $135.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations Envirogard Products Ltd. manufactures the ceramic filter cartridges, the buckets, spigots, and other components are manufactured separately and shipped together or obtained locally and assembled on-site. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Purifaaya , Caminos de Agua Ceramic filters , Tulip Table Top Filter , and Nazava Water Filter . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method Envirogard Products Ltd. manufactures the ceramic filter cartridges, the buckets, spigots, and other components are manufactured separately and shipped together or obtained locally and assembled on-site. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Users can obtain the product from Global Medic when it is distributed during natural disasters, displacements, and in refugee or internally-displaced peoples camps. It is also available for sale online from Envirogard for $135. Distributions to Date Status Partner NGO Global Medic has distributed more than 25,800 RainFresh Gravity Water Filters in Lebanon, Haiti, Somalia, the Philippines, Nepal, Vanuatu, Sudan, Pakistan, and Cambodia. Type of filter Ceramic candle (cartridge) filter Primary material of construction Silver-impregnated ceramic Additives No Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 3 L Bacteria reduction 6 (99.9999%) Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Log 3 reduction of protozoan cyst (such as cryptosporidium & Giardia) Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) <0.5 Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Maintenance schedule Scrub filter cartridges every 3-5 days Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 15,140 L Safe water storage integration 25 Design Specifications The filter setup consists of four Envirogard 7300G silver-impregnated ceramic cartridges in an upper 25L bucket over a sealed bottom 25L bucket with a spigot for dispensing water. It comes with a filter gauge for measuring the end-of-life of the ceramic candle, cleaning mesh, and the necessary hardware and pictoral instruction manual for installation. Product Schematics Technical Support Available during distributions from the partner NGO or through contacting the manufacturer. Replacement Components The four Envirogard 7300G silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridges must be replaced after filtering 15,000 L or 12 months. The upgraded 7500G filter cartridges with an activated carbon core for taste and odor reduction are available for sale in Canadian hardware stores or online in packs of four for $90 but may be available for cheaper from the distributing NGO. Lifecycle The four Envirogard 7300G silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridges must be replaced after filtering 15,000 L or 12 months. The filter gauge is used after cleanings to measure wear on the ceramic filter and indicates end-of-life. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Rainfresh P2S siphon water filter units are designed to be affordable, mass-produced, easy to assemble and use, and last a family of five drinking 3 L/day each a full year. Vetted Performance Status The four Envirogard 7300G silver-impregnated ceramic filter cartridges must be replaced after filtering 15,000 L or 12 months. The filter gauge is used after cleanings to measure wear on the ceramic filter and indicates end-of-life. Safety No known safety risks if properly used and bottom bucket remains properly sealed against recontamination. Complementary Technical Systems None Academic Research and References “Globalmedic – disaster relief,” globalmedic , 08-aug-2019.
